description: Rewrite CanonicalMapper as a two-phase (Identify → Project) mapper where the dataset's annotations define the evaluation surface via majority-vote depth.

userStories.title: Replace IssueType enum and data objects
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the new issue taxonomy in place before any mapper logic can reference it.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace IssueType values with: UNRESOLVED, COLLISION_TRIVIAL, COLLISION_AMBIGUOUS, COLLISION_CROSS_BRANCH, PREDICTION_ONLY, DATASET_ONLY
Remove old types: DEPTH_MISMATCH, SUPPRESSED_WITH_ANNOTATIONS, HIERARCHY_DEPTH_CHANGED
IssueSeverity enum unchanged (ERROR, WARNING, INFO)
Add overlap_counts field (dict[str, int] | None) to MappingIssue for token-overlap data
ResolutionOption dataclass unchanged
Update __init__.py exports to reflect new types
ruff check passes on presidio_evaluator/entity_mapping/data_objects.py
ruff check passes on presidio_evaluator/entity_mapping/__init__.py
userStories.notes: File: presidio_evaluator/entity_mapping/data_objects.py and __init__.py. This is a pure data-object change — no mapper logic yet.

userStories.title: Add get_depth helper to EntityHierarchy
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a get_depth(entity) method on EntityHierarchy so the mapper can compute majority-vote depth.
acceptanceCriteria: Add get_depth(entity: str) -> int method to EntityHierarchy that returns the depth of a canonical entity (PII=1, PERSON=2, NAME=3, FIRST_NAME=4)
Depth is derived from len(canonical_to_branch[entity])
Raises EntityNotMappedError if entity not found
Add unit test in tests/entity_mapping/test_entity_hierarchy.py for get_depth with entities at depths 2, 3, and 4
ruff check passes on hierarchy.py
Tests pass
userStories.notes: File: presidio_evaluator/entity_mapping/hierarchy.py. Small addition to existing class.

userStories.title: Scaffold new CanonicalMapper constructor
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the new CanonicalMapper constructor with only hierarchy and fuzzy_threshold params, plus internal state for eval surface locking.
acceptanceCriteria: Constructor accepts only hierarchy (dict | EntityHierarchy | None) and fuzzy_threshold (float, default 0.80) as keyword-only args
Remove labels, canonical_depth, eval_entities parameters from constructor
Add internal state: _eval_surface (set[str] | None), _eval_depth (int | None), _records (dict), _issues (list), _results_df (None)
Add eval_surface property returning set[str] (empty set if not yet computed)
Add pending property returning list of unresolved labels
Existing _auto_resolve, _auto_resolve_one, _add_labels, _stripped methods preserved (identification logic reused)
IncompleteMapping exception class preserved
ruff check passes on mapper.py
Tests pass: test that CanonicalMapper() constructs without error and eval_surface is empty
userStories.notes: File: presidio_evaluator/entity_mapping/mapper.py. Gut the old constructor, keep identification helpers.

userStories.title: Implement majority-vote depth computation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a _compute_eval_depth method that computes the weighted majority-vote depth from annotation entities.
acceptanceCriteria: Add _compute_eval_depth(results_df) method that computes round(sum(min(depth,3) * tokens) / sum(tokens))
Depth per entity comes from EntityHierarchy.get_depth()
Depth is capped at 3 per entity before weighting
Tie-breaking at 0.5: prefer deeper (more specific) depth (use math.ceil or equivalent)
Returns an int representing the eval depth
Add unit tests: dataset with all depth-3 entities returns 3; dataset with mix of depth-2 and depth-3 entities returns correct weighted result; dataset with depth-4 entities caps them to 3
ruff check passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Internal method on CanonicalMapper. Needs annotation column counts from DataFrame.

userStories.title: Implement eval surface construction and locking
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the eval surface to be computed from the majority-vote depth and locked after the first analyze() call.
acceptanceCriteria: After depth computation, eval surface = set of hierarchy nodes at that depth (from EntityHierarchy at that canonical_depth)
Eval surface stored in _eval_surface and _eval_depth
First analyze() call computes and locks the surface
Subsequent analyze() calls skip recomputation and reuse the locked surface
eval_surface property returns the locked set
Add unit test: first analyze() sets eval_surface; second analyze() with different DataFrame keeps the same eval_surface
ruff check passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Requires creating an EntityHierarchy with the computed depth to get all_canonical_entities at that depth.

userStories.title: Implement projection rules for all labels
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the projection step that maps all identified labels (annotations and predictions) onto the eval surface.
acceptanceCriteria: After identification, each label is projected onto the eval surface using hierarchy branch info
Exact match in eval surface: keep as-is
Descendant of an eval-surface entity: auto-map up to ancestor (record as COLLISION_TRIVIAL)
Unambiguous ancestor (parent of exactly 1 eval-surface entity): auto-map down (record as COLLISION_TRIVIAL)
Ambiguous ancestor (parent of N eval-surface entities): flag as COLLISION_AMBIGUOUS
Different branch from all eval-surface entities: flag as COLLISION_CROSS_BRANCH
Labels not in hierarchy: remain UNRESOLVED
Add unit tests for each projection rule with a small hierarchy and controlled DataFrame
ruff check passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Core projection logic. Uses canonical_to_branch to determine ancestor/descendant relationships.

userStories.title: Implement PREDICTION_ONLY and DATASET_ONLY detection
userStories.description: As a developer, I need issue detection for prediction-only and dataset-only entities after projection.
acceptanceCriteria: After projection, entities in predictions with no eval-surface counterpart flagged as PREDICTION_ONLY (WARNING)
After projection, eval-surface entities with no prediction mapping flagged as DATASET_ONLY (INFO)
PREDICTION_ONLY includes prediction token count
DATASET_ONLY includes annotation token count
Add unit test: model predicts EMAIL but dataset has no EMAIL → PREDICTION_ONLY
Add unit test: dataset has SSN but model never predicts it → DATASET_ONLY
ruff check passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Builds on projection step. Compare projected prediction set vs eval surface.

userStories.title: Implement issue sorting and get_issues()
userStories.description: As a developer, I need issues sorted by severity then token count so the most impactful problems surface first.
acceptanceCriteria: get_issues() returns issues sorted by severity (ERROR > WARNING > INFO), then by affected token count descending
Each MappingIssue has annotation_count and prediction_count populated from the DataFrame
UNRESOLVED issues include fuzzy suggestions at cutoff 0.40
Add unit test: multiple issues of different severities and token counts are returned in correct order
ruff check passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Replaces old _detect_unresolved, _detect_prediction_only, _detect_depth_mismatch, _detect_suppressed_with_annotations methods.

userStories.title: Implement token-overlap ranking for ambiguous collisions
userStories.description: As a developer, I need COLLISION_AMBIGUOUS suggestions ranked by actual per-row token overlap.
acceptanceCriteria: For each COLLISION_AMBIGUOUS issue, scan DataFrame rows where prediction == the ambiguous label
Count co-occurrences with each candidate annotation label from the eval surface
Store overlap counts in MappingIssue.overlap_counts (dict[str, int])
ResolutionOption suggestions ordered by overlap count descending
Add unit test: prediction PERSON with annotations NAME (100 tokens), TITLE (10 tokens), USERNAME (2 tokens) → suggestions ranked NAME > TITLE > USERNAME
ruff check passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Scans the results_df to count per-row annotation/prediction co-occurrences.

userStories.title: Wire up analyze() end-to-end
userStories.description: As a developer, I need analyze() to run the full pipeline: identify → compute depth → lock surface → project → detect issues → sort.
acceptanceCriteria: analyze(results_df) discovers labels from annotation and prediction columns
Runs identification (reuses existing _auto_resolve logic)
Computes majority-vote depth and locks eval surface (first call only)
Runs projection on all identified labels
Detects PREDICTION_ONLY, DATASET_ONLY, COLLISION_* issues
Sorts issues by severity then token count
Returns self for chaining
Manual records (from prior map() calls) are preserved across analyze() calls
Issues are cleared and recomputed on each analyze() call
Add integration test: full analyze() with a realistic DataFrame covering exact matches, trivial collisions, and an unresolved label
ruff check passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: This wires together US-004 through US-009 into the analyze() method.

userStories.title: Update map() with issue re-evaluation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need map() to validate atomically, apply mappings, and re-evaluate which issues are resolved.
acceptanceCriteria: map() validates all entries before applying any: raises ValueError on invalid canonical values
Supports remap ({'X': 'Y'}), suppress ({'X': None}), keep-as-FP ({'X': 'X'})
After applying, the mapper re-evaluates and removes resolved issues from _issues list
map() can override an already-resolved label (corrects auto-fix decisions)
Returns self for chaining
Add unit test: map() resolves a PREDICTION_ONLY warning, then get_issues() no longer contains it
Add unit test: map() with invalid value raises ValueError and applies nothing
ruff check passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Builds on existing map() logic but adds issue re-evaluation after applying.

userStories.title: Implement blocking in get_mapped_results_dataframe()
userStories.description: As a developer, I need get_mapped_results_dataframe() to block on WARNING+ issues and output original_ columns.
acceptanceCriteria: Raises IncompleteMapping if any ERROR or WARNING issues remain unresolved
INFO issues (COLLISION_TRIVIAL, DATASET_ONLY) do not block
Exception message lists the unresolved issue types and labels
When no blocking issues: returns DataFrame with annotation and prediction rewritten to eval-surface entities
Suppressed labels become 'O' in both columns
Adds original_annotation and original_prediction columns preserving raw labels
Other columns (sentence_id, token, start_indices) unchanged
Add unit test: calling with unresolved WARNING raises IncompleteMapping
Add unit test: calling after resolving all warnings returns correct DataFrame with original_ columns
ruff check passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: File: mapper.py. Updates existing get_mapped_results_dataframe().

userStories.title: Implement logging for all resolution decisions
userStories.description: As a developer, I need structured logging at INFO/WARNING for every resolution decision.
acceptanceCriteria: EXACT resolutions logged at INFO: '[EXACT] EMAIL_ADDRESS → EMAIL_ADDRESS'
FUZZY resolutions logged at INFO with score: '[FUZZY 87%] EMAILADRES → EMAIL_ADDRESS'
COUNTRY resolutions logged at INFO: '[COUNTRY] GERMAN_PASSPORT_NUMBER → PASSPORT'
COUNTRY_FALLBACK logged at INFO with warning marker: '[COUNTRY-FALLBACK] ... → NATIONAL_ID ⚠ document type not recognized'
Auto-fixes logged at INFO: '[AUTO-FIX] FIRST_NAME → NAME (descendant projected to eval-surface entity)'
Manual overrides logged: '[MANUAL] MY_LABEL → PERSON'
Suppressions logged: '[NONE] EXTRA_LABEL → None (suppressed from evaluation)'
Unresolved labels logged at WARNING: '[UNRESOLVED] {label} — no automatic match found'
Summary INFO line after analysis with depth, surface entities, resolution counts, auto-fixes, issues count
Logger name is presidio_evaluator.entity_mapping
ruff check passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Update existing logging calls in _auto_resolve_one and add new logging in projection step.

userStories.title: Implement resolve_interactively()
userStories.description: As a developer, I need resolve_interactively() to prompt only for WARNING+ issues with ranked suggestions.
acceptanceCriteria: resolve_interactively(prompt_fn=input) iterates over WARNING+ issues only
For each issue shows: issue type, affected token count, ranked suggestions (with overlap counts for COLLISION_AMBIGUOUS)
Prompt accepts: suggestion number, free-text entity name, or NONE to suppress
Re-prompts on invalid input
No-op when there are no WARNING+ issues
Returns self for chaining
Add unit test with injected prompt_fn that auto-answers: verify issue is resolved after interactive session
ruff check passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: New method. Uses prompt_fn for testability.

userStories.title: Implement render_html() audit table
userStories.description: As a developer, I need render_html() to show a summary bar and detail table with badge colors.
acceptanceCriteria: Summary bar shows: auto-discovered depth, counts per issue type, total auto-fixes applied
Detail table has one row per raw label, sorted by severity then token count
Badge colours: exact match = green, COLLISION_TRIVIAL = blue, UNRESOLVED = red, PREDICTION_ONLY = grey, DATASET_ONLY = amber, COLLISION_AMBIGUOUS/CROSS_BRANCH = yellow/orange
Cross-branch overlaps with high token counts show systematic misprediction insights
Uses IPython.display.HTML in Jupyter; falls back to plain text
Never raises (catches ImportError for IPython)
Fully self-contained HTML (inline styles, no external CSS/JS)
Can be called at any point reflecting current state
Add unit test: render_html() does not raise when IPython is unavailable
ruff check passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Rewrite of existing _build_html. New layout: summary bar + detail table.
